payload.text: build this with modern graphics and color patterns

**Defining the Application Scope**

I'm currently focused on defining the scope of this React application, specifically a Rental Management System. I'm taking the provided screenshots of feature lists and mockups and I'm diving deep to understand how all of the components will function together. I'm prioritizing the core functionalities and making sure all the products are rent-able.


**Outlining Functional Components**

I'm now outlining key functional components. I'm focusing on custom durations, availability calendars, and the customer portal for order review and payment. I'm also addressing pickup/return scheduling, and notifications to streamline the rental lifecycle. Further development includes payment gateway integration, delivery management, and flexible invoicing. Then the pricelist management, and the handling of any return or delay. Finally, reports and dashboards will be considered for revenue and the most popular rentals.


**Structuring the Frontend Architecture**

I'm now deeply involved in the application's structure. I'm finalizing component locations and routing. I'm planning shared interfaces with `types.ts`, and I'm organizing components such as `Sidebar`, `Navbar`, and others within their respective folders. I'm establishing mock data generation via Gemini and localStorage integration since a real backend isn't available. A Gemini Assistant is planned. I'm focusing on the colors, the sidebar, the responsive layout, and the visualization of data.

payload.text: There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Considering Authentication Methods**

I'm currently focused on implementing dual user authentication: Admin and Customer. My current plan involves a unified login/signup page offering role selection. Successful authentication will then trigger role-based redirection: Admins to their dashboard, and Customers to their respective areas.


**Refining the UI**

I'm now zeroing in on UI refinements for the authentication flow and shop views. I'm aiming for a clean, split-screen or centered card design for login/signup, with animated transitions. I plan to remove any "switch role" buttons from the navigation to prevent mode-jumping. I am considering CSS transitions and transforms for the animations. I also decided to manage user authentication in `App.tsx` and create a `Auth.tsx` component.

entries.description: Add UserRole enum for authentication differentiation.
New sleek authentication view for Login and Signup with role selection and animations.
Update App to handle global authentication state and role-based redirection.
Add logout functionality to Sidebar.
Remove "Switch to Customer Portal" to enforce strict role differentiation.
Remove Admin Portal jump option and replace with Logout in Customer Shop.

payload.text: There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Refining Authentication Flow**

I'm currently working to refine the authentication flow. My focus is now on implementing distinct user roles (Admin and Customer). The plan involves integrating a role selection option on the login/signup page. Upon successful authentication, the user will be seamlessly redirected to the appropriate Admin or Customer-specific pages.


**Verifying Flow Integrity**

I'm now cross-checking the entire flow to confirm its integrity, as requested. I've re-examined the key files: `App.tsx`, `Auth.tsx`, `CustomerShop.tsx`, and `Sidebar.tsx`, along with the navbar to verify correct role-based routing and removal of interface-jumping options. Authentication and redirection appear to be functioning as expected based on the role selected at login. I'm focusing on confirming the expected behavior.


**Improving Visual Experience**

I'm now focusing on refining the visual experience. I'm prioritizing smooth, professional transitions. I will ensure all relevant CSS for animations is present in `index.html`. Furthermore, I will revisit `Auth.tsx` to enhance the animations there, too. I'm verifying the role-based logic in `App.tsx` and ensuring that it remains airtight, and will check for any "jumps" between roles.


**Confirming Full Integration**

My current concern is ensuring all relevant files are fully integrated and not truncated, as that can lead to errors. I am now double-checking the code blocks I've written to ensure their completeness. I am reviewing the updates to `index.html`, `App.tsx`, `Auth.tsx`, `types.ts`, `CustomerShop.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, and `Sidebar.tsx`. I will also create a "profile" element in the admin dashboard to confirm who is currently logged in. I will pay special attention to the requirement for no role "jumps".

Remove any ability to jump to admin, ensuring it is a standalone customer experience.
